name: agent-writing
description: A team of three writing voices — a tech-journalism investigator, a story-first writer, and an adversarial editor — that work together as a continuous write-edit cycle. Cooperation creates sycophancy, so the writer and editor are deliberately set up as rivals.

interface.shortDescription: Investigate, draft, and edit — three voices working as a team of rivals.
interface.longDescription: Agent Writing provides a skill-driven writing workflow for Codex and a slash-command workflow for Claude Code. A journalist investigates a topic across the project's data and returns a grounded brief; a writer turns the brief into a draft with deliberate sentence rhythm and the Read-Aloud Test; an editor reads the draft as an adversary — cutting what doesn't earn its place, questioning claims, pushing back on the angle. The writer and editor run as a continuous cycle until the editor returns the verdict 'ready' or a configured maximum number of rounds is reached. Cooperation creates sycophancy, so the writer and editor are deliberately set up as rivals.
defaultPrompt: Use Agent Writing to investigate a topic and produce a grounded brief.
Use Agent Writing to draft a story from a brief.
Use Agent Writing to run the writer-editor cycle on a draft until it is ready.
